item.title: Flood damages over 200km roads in Ctg
item.description: Trails of destruction have emerged as floodwater recedes in southern, central and northern Bangladesh, with official estimates showing that more than 200km of roads were damaged in Chattogram division alone between July 6 and 12......

item.title: Protests over HSC exams continue
item.description: Pressing a set of demands that included the education minister’s resignation, students continued their protests on Wednesday by blocking major highways across the country and key intersections in Dhaka, halting traffic and further disrupting daily life and business activities......

item.title: Zero tolerance to extremism: PM
item.description: The second session of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad was prorogued on Wednesday, with prime minister Tarique Rahman pledging that the government would not tolerate any form of extremism or radicalism and calling for cooperation from the opposition to build a Bangladesh where democratic rights would not be undermined in the name of any ideology......City& Local Guides

item.title: July Martyrs’ Day today
item.description: Bangladesh is observing July Martyrs’ Day today, marking the death anniversary of Abu Sayeed, the first protester killed during the July-August 2024 mass uprising, and paying tribute to all those who lost their lives in the movement......

item.title: Teacher, infrastructure crises plague technical education
item.description: More than five decades after independence, the country’s technical and vocational education sector remains plagued by deep-rooted challenges that stifle its ability to produce skilled manpower, despite repeated reform pledges by successive governments......

item.title: Suspicious transactions in banks surge: BFIU
item.description: The number of suspicious transaction and activity reports related to money laundering surged by 74 per cent in Bangladesh in the 2024-25 fiscal year, according to the Bangladesh Financial Intelligence Unit......

item.title: KDS Group regains control of Al-Arafah
item.description: Bangladesh Bank on Wednesday restored sponsor representation by allowing the bank’s founding shareholders, led by KDS Group, to regain control......Bangladeshnews updates

item.title: KSA keen to invest in Bangladesh’s transport, logistics
item.description: Saudi Arabia has expressed strong interest in expanding its investment footprint in Bangladesh, particularly in the transport, logistics and maritime sectors, as both countries seek to deepen economic cooperation......

item.title: BEPZA contributes 17.51pc to national exports in FY26
item.description: Factories inside various zones under the Bangladesh Export Processing Zones Authority contributed 17.51 per cent during the financial year 2025-26, according to BEPZA data......

item.title: BB eases foreign borrowing rules for foreign-owned firms
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has eased foreign borrowing rules for fully foreign-owned companies, allowing them to borrow funds from their parent companies, affiliated companies and shareholders easily......

item.title: ZnZ unveils autumn, winter collection at 18th Fabric Week
item.description: Zaber & Zubair Fabrics Limited unveiled its autumn and winter collection for 2027-28 at the ongoing 18th edition of Fabric Week, with a strong focus on sustainable materials, circularity and textile innovation......History

item.title: BSEC seeks to make IPO process transparent
item.description: The Bangladesh Securities and Exchange Commission on Wednesday held a stakeholder consultation meeting aimed at making the country’s Initial Public Offering process more transparent, accountable, efficient and internationally standard-compliant......

item.title: NBR asks BB to make BIN must for business bank accounts
item.description: The National Board of Revenue asked the Bangladesh Bank to make value added tax registration, or business identification number, mandatory for opening and operating all business current and short-term deposit accounts as part of efforts to expand the VAT net and increase revenue collection......

item.title: Stocks extend rally
item.description: Stocks today extended winning streak for a fifth consecutive trading session, buoyed by improved investor confidence, supportive policy developments and a positive near-term market outlook......News

item.title: GP net profit strengthens in Q2 despite down revenue
item.description: Mobile phone operator Grameenphone posted a net profit after taxes of Tk 759 crore in the second quarter of 2026, reflecting resilience in earnings despite a three per cent year‑on‑year decline in revenues......

item.title: BTMA seeks BB support to revive weak textile mills
item.description: The country’s textile millers on Wednesday urged Bangladesh Bank to introduce a set of policy measures to keep the country’s export-oriented primary textile industry afloat, arguing that timely support would also help reduce the risk of non-performing loans in the banking sector......

item.title: BPPA begins 5-day e-GP training to boost public procurement
item.description: The Bangladesh Public Procurement Authority has continued its nationwide training programme on the electronic Government Procurement, e-GP system, as part of its ongoing efforts to strengthen the capacity of key public procurement stakeholders......

item.title: Meta staff allege discriminatory AI-driven layoffs
item.description: Twenty-six Meta employees have filed a lawsuit accusing the tech giant of using artificial intelligence to select workers for mass layoffs, a claim strongly denied by the trillion dollar company......Bangladeshnews updates

item.title: China economic growth hits slowest pace in 3 years
item.description: China’s economy grew at its weakest pace in more than three years during the second quarter, data showed Wednesday, missing expectations even as strong exports driven by the global AI boom helped offset trade disruptions caused by the Middle East war......

item.title: Generative AI’s power sparks fears of dumbing humans down
item.description: Generative AI chatbots capable of writing emails and computer code, translating, organising a trip or coming up with gift ideas are now readily available, prompting some to ask whether human brainpower could suffer for lack of use......

item.title: Australian PM says to enact laws to govern AI
item.description: Australia will enact laws to regulate how artificial intelligence data centres use power and water, and to protect creative copyright, prime minister Anthony Albanese said Wednesday......

item.title: Energy insecurity calls for action, structural reforms
item.description: A BLEAK energy outlook appears forthcoming in the 2026–27 financial year. Already grappling with a severe power shortage, often exceeding 3,000 megawatts during the summer and the high cost of gas imports from the volatile spot market, there appears no definitive solution to the problem around. Despite a higher budgetary allocation for the sector, the country......

item.title: Govt should align skills with labour market demand
item.description: THE country observed World Youth Skills Day on July 15 amid a youth unemployment rate of about 9.39 per cent, with underemployment among educated youth and university graduates remaining severe because of short-sighted skills programmes that have failed to equip them with the skills needed for domestic and global labour markets. The skills development system......Maps

item.title: Crucial negotiations ahead
item.description: BANGLADESH is set to engage with India in crucial political-level negotiations over the renewal of the Ganges Treaty, signed between the two neighbours in 1996 and due to expire in December, apparently amid declining flow in the common river and differing positions over the very formula for water sharing......

item.title: Where does the referendum stand?
item.description: AFTER the budget is passed, usually the last activities of the budget session of the parliament are not very lively. Many times, the session is limited to completing formalities. But the session on July 13 was a complete exception. The sharp differences between the treasury and the opposition benches on the issue of constitutional amendment created a heated......

item.title: Invisible diplomats
item.description: BANGLADESH’S most influential representatives abroad rarely occupy diplomatic missions or participate in international summits. They are more likely to be found on construction sites in the Gulf, in factories across Malaysia and Singapore, in hospitals and care homes in Europe, or driving vehicles and working in restaurants around the world. Every day, millions of......GeographicReference

item.title: Excellence beyond rankings
item.description: EVERY year, the publication of global university rankings sparks a familiar debate in Bangladesh. Headlines focus on whether the country’s universities have climbed or slipped a few places in the QS World University Rankings or the Times Higher Education rankings. The latest editions were no exception. The University of Dhaka appeared around the 600th position......

item.title: AL leader found dead on Jashore riverbank
item.description: Police recovered the body of a local Awami League leader from the bank of the Betrabati River in Jhikargacha upazila of Jashore on Wednesday afternoon......News

item.title: US strikes Iran again, hits ship trying to break port blockade
item.description: A US aircraft fired on and disabled an unladen oil tanker on Wednesday as it reportedly tried to break the naval blockade of Iran’s ports, the US military said......

item.title: Messi's Argentina stun England to reach World Cup final
item.description: Lautaro Martinez scored a 92nd minute winner as Lionel Messi inspired World Cup holders Argentina to a stunning comeback to beat England 2-1 on Wednesday and set up a final with European champions Spain....

item.title: Aminbazar waste-to-energy project cancellation sought
item.description: The Bangladesh Working Group on Ecology and Development on Wednesday called for the cancellation of the Aminbazar Waste-to-Energy Power Plant project, describing the facility as one of the country’s most expensive and environmentally high-risk power ventures......News

item.title: 3 charged with ex-JnU JCD leader killing
item.description: Police have submitted a charge sheet against three people in connection with the murder of former Jagannath University unit Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal leader Md Jobayed Hossen, concluding that the killing was the result of a month-long premeditated plan motivated by a love affair......

item.title: BUET achieves success in developing ‘Pragati v1’
item.description: The department of electrical and electronic engineering at Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology has achieved a milestone by taping out ‘Pragati v1’, a 100pc open-source 32-bit RISC-V processor......

item.title: CU distributes relief to flood victims at Hathazari
item.description: The Chittagong University administration on Tuesday distributed emergency relief supplies to helpless and destitute families affected by the recent floods caused by continuous heavy rainfall at Hathazari in Chattogram......

item.title: 38th nat’l age group swimming, diving championship concludes
item.description: The four-day 38th national age group swimming and diving championship concluded at the National Swimming Complex of Mirpur in Dhaka on Tuesday......Bangladeshnews updates

item.title: Exhibition remembers lives sacrificed in July uprising
item.description: A special photography exhibition by photojournalist Jibon Ahmed began on Wednesday at the Bangladesh National Museum in remembrance of the July uprising and the lives sacrificed to topple the authoritarian Awami League regime......

item.title: All madrassahs asked to display banners for ‘Natun Kuri Sports’ prog
item.description: The Bangladesh Madrassah Education Board has instructed all madrassahs across the country to display banners promoting the government’s ‘Natun Kuri Sports 2026-2027’ programme to ensure wider publicity and encourage student participation......

item.title: European envoys meet aviation minister over Airbus deal
item.description: Civil aviation and tourism minister Afroza Khanam and state minister M Rashiduzzaman Millat held a meeting with top diplomats from France, the United Kingdom, Spain, Germany and the European Union at the conference room of the ministry in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: 18 DU students receive Champion Sports Award
item.description: Eighteen students of Dhaka University’s Bijay Ekattar Hall have been honoured with the Champion Sports Award for their performances and achievements in various national and international sports competitions......City& Local Guides

item.title: BSA holds `Barshay Nazrul’
item.description: The Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy held a cultural evening featuring the poems and songs of national poet Kazi Nazrul Islam titled Barshay Nazrul at the National Theatre Hall of the academy in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: SEU becomes champions at TNRS
item.description: Students from the department of computer science and engineering at Southeast University have recently secured championship titles in two competition segments at the Techathon Nationals and Rover Summit 2026, organised by the IUT Robotics Society......

item.title: Karail drowns in misery again
item.description: For Helena Akhter, every spell of monsoon rain revives an old fear. This year, it turned into reality......

item.title: Air pollution killed 242 people a day in 2021: study
item.description: Two hundred and forty-two people died each day in Bangladesh in 2021 due to air pollution, causing an estimated $23 billion in economic losses that year, according to a study by researchers at Jahangirnagar University......

item.title: BB’s all-male AI training selection draws criticism
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has selected 30 officials for a specialised artificial intelligence training programme without including a single woman, despite women accounting for nearly one-fifth of the applicants......ExecutiveBranch

item.title: 14 lakh tonnes of diesel import without tender proposed
item.description: The energy division has sent a proposal to the cabinet committee on economic affairs, seeking approval for importing 14.75 lakh tonnes diesel without tenders, said officials of the energy division......

item.title: Nahid alleges deliberate power outage at DU
item.description: National Citizen Party convener Nahid Islam on Wednesday alleged that the power outage during a July memorial programme at Dhaka University was deliberate......

item.title: US expands Iran oil, crypto sanctions
item.description: The United States launched a wave of strikes against Iran on Wednesday, after it reimposed a naval blockade in a return to war between the two foes......

item.title: Two years on, families still search for missing July fighters
item.description: Two years after the July uprising, many martyrs still remain unaccounted for, as families of missing activists continue searching for their loved ones while graves dug during the mass movement lie unidentified in graveyards......WesternEuropeans

item.title: Int’l rights bodies call for release of detained journalists
item.description: Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch, along with several other international rights bodies, in a statement on Wednesday called for the immediate release of detained journalists Farzana Rupa, Shakil Ahmed, Mozammel Babu and Shyamal Dutta......
